title: Service refuses to start: 'already running' but it isn't
description_md:
The `queue-worker` daemon is down, but every attempt to start it with `queue-workerctl start` refuses, insisting it's 'already running' — yet nothing is actually running. The box was hard-powered-off last week. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), work out why the control script thinks it's already up, clear that, and get the worker running.

You're done when the `queue-worker` process is actually running.
